description:
Goal: make compiler2 native codegen compiler2-owned end to end, so the compiler2 route no longer depends on old-world semantic codegen or a second type owner before JIT/AOT.

Current conclusion from the landed code:
- `Compiler2::compile_root_jit`, `run_root_jit`, and `compile_root_aot` now consume compiler2-owned native backend state directly through `NativeProgram(root)` plus the one world-owned compiler2 `Types`.
- copied planner-shaped baggage (`SpecPlan`, `SpecRegistry`, synthetic `SpecKey`, widened `return_tys`) is gone from the compiler2-native handoff.
- compiler2-native no longer re-projects runtime membership through a fake legacy `Ty` world; direct runtime tests and cached receive dispatch use the shared first-class `RuntimeTypePredicate`.
- the remaining migration problem above this seam is cutover and contract comparison, tracked by `fz-rh2.9` once its other blockers clear.

Contained follow-up outside this arc: `fz-rh2.15` tracks the last compiler2-native pocket still fabricating old-world semantic extern payloads on `fz_ir::ExternDecl`.
title: Compiler2 native backends: bring native codegen in-house and delete old-world backend dead weight
